questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other traffic is present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a full stop and wait until the light turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe
Continue without stopping
Stop and wait for a green light
Yield only to pedestrians
questions.explanation: A flashing red light works like a stop sign: stop completely, then go when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The light is about to turn green
Stop and remain stopped
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop if it is safe rather than rushing through.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
choices: A red octagon
A yellow diamond
A white rectangle
An orange triangle
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon, a shape used for no other sign so it is recognizable from any angle.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely before proceeding
Stop and wait for a green light
The signal is broken and should be ignored
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and cross or proceed carefully.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal mean?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow with the right-of-way
You must stop before turning
You may only turn if the arrow flashes
Oncoming traffic also has a green light
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected movement in the arrow's direction while conflicting traffic is stopped.

questions.prompt: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign generally indicate?
choices: A warning about conditions ahead
A regulatory speed limit
The end of a highway
A pedestrian-only zone
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards or changes in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the road means:
choices: You may not cross it to pass
You may pass when the road is clear
The road ahead ends
Two-way traffic merges
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side prohibits passing across it because of limited sight distance or other hazards.

questions.prompt: What color are construction and work zone signs in Oregon?
choices: Orange
Blue
Green
Brown
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and work zones, where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power outage, you should treat the intersection as:
choices: A four-way stop
A green light
A yield with no need to stop
Closed to all traffic
questions.explanation: A dark or non-functioning signal should be treated as an all-way stop, with normal right-of-way rules applying.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
Only vehicles behind you
No one, because you have a green light
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left turn requires you to yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing your path.

questions.prompt: In Oregon, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
Never
Only between dawn and dusk
Without stopping if no one is around
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after stopping completely and yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic, unless a sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked or unmarked crosswalk?
choices: Pedestrians
Drivers turning right
The vehicle going straight
Whoever arrives first
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in both marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a driveway or parking lot, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road and to pedestrians on the sidewalk
Proceed because you have the right-of-way
Honk and merge
Stop only if a sign requires it
questions.explanation: Traffic on the roadway and pedestrians on the sidewalk have the right-of-way over vehicles entering from a driveway.

questions.prompt: You must stop for a pedestrian who is crossing with a white cane or guide dog because:
choices: They may be blind and always have the right-of-way
It is only a courtesy
They are required to wait for you
Only if they are in a marked crosswalk
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a blind pedestrian, who always has the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle that arrived first or to the one on your right
Always go first
Speed through quickly
Stop and wait for all other traffic
questions.explanation: Without signs or signals, yield to whoever arrived first, and to the driver on your right if you arrive together.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, you should signal:
choices: Well in advance so others know your intention
Only as you begin the turn
Only if other cars are nearby
After you complete the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers and pedestrians time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles approach a roundabout, you should:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Enter quickly before the other car
Stop in the roundabout to let others in
Always yield to vehicles entering from your right
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right-of-way over vehicles entering.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in an Oregon residential district is:
choices: 25 mph
20 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Oregon's statutory speed limit in residential districts is 25 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in an Oregon business district is:
choices: 20 mph
25 mph
30 mph
15 mph
questions.explanation: The statutory speed in a business district is 20 mph unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: Oregon's Basic Speed Rule requires you to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for current conditions
Always drive exactly the posted speed limit
Never drive below the posted limit
Match the speed of surrounding traffic
questions.explanation: The Basic Speed Rule means you must adjust your speed for weather, traffic, and road conditions, even below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: The recommended minimum following distance under good conditions is:
choices: At least three seconds
One car length
One second
Ten feet
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough space and time to react and stop safely in good conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog, you should adjust your following distance by:
choices: Increasing it to four seconds or more
Keeping it the same
Reducing it to stay close
Following the car's tail lights closely
questions.explanation: Poor conditions lengthen stopping distance, so you need more space ahead, four seconds or more.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, your braking distance:
choices: Increases much faster than your speed
Stays the same
Decreases
Increases by exactly the same amount as speed
questions.explanation: Braking distance grows rapidly with speed, so a small speed increase greatly lengthens the distance needed to stop.

questions.prompt: The statutory speed limit in most Oregon school zones when children are present is:
choices: 20 mph
25 mph
30 mph
15 mph
questions.explanation: School zones generally have a 20 mph limit to protect children near schools.

questions.prompt: When another driver is tailgating you, the safest response is to:
choices: Increase your following distance from the vehicle ahead
Brake suddenly to warn them
Speed up to match them
Move into their blind spot
questions.explanation: Leaving more room ahead gives you a cushion to brake gently so the tailgater has time to react.

questions.prompt: Your total stopping distance is made up of:
choices: Perception and reaction distance plus braking distance
Only braking distance
Only reaction time
The length of the vehicle plus braking distance
questions.explanation: Stopping distance includes the distance traveled while you perceive and react, plus the distance to brake to a stop.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Forces others to brake and pass, increasing crash risk
Always saves fuel safely
Is never a violation
Improves overall traffic flow
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts traffic flow and causes others to make risky passing maneuvers.

questions.prompt: You should generally pass other vehicles on the:
choices: Left
Right
Shoulder
Either side equally
questions.explanation: Passing is normally done on the left, with passing on the right allowed only in specific situations.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to pass another vehicle when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
The road is straight and clear
A broken yellow line is on your side
Traffic ahead is moving slowly
questions.explanation: You may not pass when your view is blocked by a hill or curve, because oncoming traffic could appear suddenly.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, slower traffic should:
choices: Keep to the right
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Slower drivers keep right so faster traffic can pass safely on the left.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
Only check your rearview mirror
Honk and move over
Rely on your side mirror alone
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show everything, so a shoulder check clears the blind spot before you change lanes.

questions.prompt: After you pass a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Immediately after pulling alongside it
The vehicle behind flashes its lights
You reach the next intersection
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to return safely without cutting it off.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly to let it pass
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move toward the centerline
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Speeding up while being passed is dangerous; hold or reduce your speed so the pass can be completed safely.

questions.prompt: You may not pass within how far of a railroad crossing or intersection?
choices: You may not pass at intersections or railroad crossings
Within 10 feet only
Passing is always allowed there
Only at night
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited at intersections and railroad crossings because of the high risk of conflict.

questions.prompt: A single broken white line between lanes means:
choices: You may change lanes when it is safe
Lane changes are prohibited
Traffic flows in opposite directions
The lane is closed
questions.explanation: A broken white line separates same-direction lanes and may be crossed when safe to change lanes.

questions.prompt: When choosing a lane on a road with a center left-turn lane, you should use that lane only to:
choices: Prepare for or make a left turn
Pass slower traffic
Drive straight through
Park temporarily
questions.explanation: A center two-way left-turn lane is for turning left only, not for through travel or passing.

questions.prompt: Weaving in and out of traffic to gain a few car lengths is unsafe because it:
choices: Increases your blind-spot exposure and crash risk
Always saves significant time
Is encouraged in heavy traffic
Improves traffic flow for everyone
questions.explanation: Frequent lane changes raise the chance of a collision while saving very little time.

questions.prompt: Under Oregon's Move Over law, when you approach a stopped vehicle with flashing lights you must:
choices: Change lanes away from it, or slow to at least 5 mph below the posted speed
Maintain your speed
Stop in your lane
Speed up to pass quickly
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires moving over a lane or, if you cannot, slowing at least 5 mph below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: When you must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm:
choices: Stop until the lights stop flashing and the arm folds back
Pass slowly on the left
Honk and continue
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: You must remain stopped for a school bus with flashing red lights until it is safe to proceed and the arm retracts.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them room and treat them as a legal road user
Force them onto the shoulder
Pass closely to save space
Honk to make them move
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same rights and duties as drivers and need room to ride safely.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have big blind spots, so you should avoid:
choices: Lingering alongside them where the driver cannot see you
Passing them at all
Driving in front of them ever
Using your signals near them
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you, so do not linger in those blind spots.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles easy to miss in traffic?
choices: They are small and can be hidden in blind spots
They are always speeding
They have no lights
They never use the full lane
questions.explanation: Motorcycles' small size makes them easy to overlook, so look twice before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making a right turn may:
choices: Swing wide and need extra room, so do not squeeze beside them
Always turn from the far left lane
Turn faster than cars
Complete the turn without slowing
questions.explanation: Trucks often swing wide to make turns, so never try to pass on the side they are turning toward.

questions.prompt: When you open your car door after parking on a street, you should:
choices: Check for approaching bicyclists and traffic first
Open it quickly to save time
Rely on your mirror only
Assume the lane is clear
questions.explanation: Checking before opening your door prevents 'dooring' a passing cyclist or being hit by traffic.

questions.prompt: In neighborhoods, near schools, and at bus stops you should:
choices: Slow down and watch for children who may enter the street
Maintain highway speeds
Use your horn constantly
Assume children will stay on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Children can be unpredictable, so reduce speed and stay alert around schools, parks, and bus stops.

questions.prompt: A bicyclist may legally use a full travel lane when:
choices: The lane is too narrow to share safely
Never, under any circumstance
Only on bike paths
Only at night
questions.explanation: Cyclists may take the full lane when it is too narrow to share, and drivers must give them room.

questions.prompt: On a divided highway, when must you stop for a school bus stopped on the opposite side?
choices: You do not have to stop if a median or barrier divides the road
Always, in both directions
Only at night
Only if students are crossing
questions.explanation: On a divided highway, traffic on the other side of a physical barrier is not required to stop for the bus.

questions.prompt: In Oregon, the legal BAC limit for drivers 21 and older is:
choices: 0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Oregon, the legal blood alcohol limit is:
choices: Any measurable amount is illegal (zero tolerance)
0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Oregon has a zero-tolerance law, so any amount of alcohol is illegal for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Oregon drivers age 18 and older may use a mobile phone while driving only:
choices: With a hands-free accessory
By holding it briefly
When stopped at a red light
While texting at low speed
questions.explanation: Drivers 18 and older may only use a mobile device with hands-free technology while driving.

questions.prompt: Drivers under 18 in Oregon may use a cell phone while driving:
choices: Not at all, even hands-free
Only with a hands-free device
While stopped in traffic
For navigation only
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 are prohibited from using any mobile device while driving, even hands-free.

questions.prompt: Under Oregon's Implied Consent law, refusing a breath or blood test after a DUII arrest results in:
choices: An automatic license suspension
A warning only
No consequences
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: By driving in Oregon you consent to testing, so refusal leads to an automatic suspension separate from criminal charges.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid driving impaired is to:
choices: Plan a sober ride before you drink
Drink coffee before driving
Wait 15 minutes after drinking
Roll down the windows
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from your body, so arrange a sober driver, rideshare, or alternative in advance.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Alcohol, marijuana, and some prescription or over-the-counter medicines
Only alcohol
Only illegal drugs
Only marijuana
questions.explanation: Many substances, including legal medicines, can impair judgment, vision, and reaction time.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes:
choices: Texting, eating, or anything that takes your attention from driving
Only using a cell phone
Only eating
Only talking to passengers
questions.explanation: Any activity that diverts your eyes, hands, or mind from driving is a distraction and increases crash risk.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Improving your focus
Sharpening your vision
Having no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reaction time and impair the judgment needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: The safest way to handle your phone while driving is to:
choices: Set up navigation before you start and put the phone away
Hold it low so police cannot see
Use it only on the highway
Glance at it at stoplights
questions.explanation: Setting everything before you drive and putting the phone away keeps your attention on the road.

questions.prompt: Oregon law requires you to turn on your headlights:
choices: From sunset to sunrise and whenever you cannot see clearly 1,000 feet ahead
Only on the highway
Only when it is fully dark
Only in the city
questions.explanation: Headlights are required between sunset and sunrise and any time visibility drops below 1,000 feet.

questions.prompt: A good rule of thumb in Oregon is to turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: Your windshield wipers are running
You are on a one-way street
You are parked
The radio is on
questions.explanation: Turning on headlights with your wipers makes you visible to others in rain, snow, or mist.

questions.prompt: In dense fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while driving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams are safer in fog.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Slam on the brakes
Steer the opposite way
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering gently toward your intended path helps regain control.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road typically freezes first in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Hills
Tunnels
Parking lots
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before other road surfaces.

questions.prompt: If your tires begin to hydroplane on water, you should:
choices: Ease off the accelerator and hold the wheel steady
Brake hard immediately
Turn the wheel sharply
Accelerate to push through the water
questions.explanation: Letting off the gas and keeping the wheel straight lets the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
Maintain your normal speed
Use high beams
Follow other cars closely to see better
questions.explanation: Rain reduces traction and visibility, so reduce speed and leave extra space ahead.

questions.prompt: If conditions become too dangerous to keep driving, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on your hazard lights
Stop in your lane
Drive faster to get through
Turn off all your lights
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the road with hazards on keeps you out of traffic until conditions improve.

questions.prompt: At night you should drive at a speed that lets you stop within:
choices: The distance lit by your headlights
Twice your headlight range
One block
The length of the road ahead you assume is clear
questions.explanation: Do not overdrive your headlights; you must be able to stop within the distance you can see.

questions.prompt: Properly inflated tires and good wiper blades are especially important in Oregon because:
choices: The climate is often wet, reducing traction and visibility
They make the car faster
They are required only in summer
They improve fuel economy on dry roads
questions.explanation: Oregon's frequent rain makes good tires and wipers essential for traction and clear vision.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
Toward the center of the road
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill stops the car from rolling into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb so the tire rests against it
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Either direction
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turning wheels away lets the tire roll back against the curb and stop the vehicle.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park or stop?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant or blocking a crosswalk
In a marked parking space
In a driveway you own
In a metered space
questions.explanation: Parking that blocks hydrants, crosswalks, driveways, or ramps is prohibited because it endangers others.

questions.prompt: In Oregon, a child must ride in a child safety seat until they reach:
choices: 40 pounds
20 pounds
60 pounds
30 pounds
questions.explanation: Children must be in a child safety seat until they weigh 40 pounds or reach the seat's upper weight limit.

questions.prompt: In Oregon, infants must ride in a rear-facing seat until they are at least:
choices: 2 years old
1 year old
6 months old
4 years old
questions.explanation: Oregon requires infants to ride rear-facing until at least age two for the best crash protection.

questions.prompt: In Oregon, a child must use a booster seat until they are:
choices: 4 feet 9 inches tall or age eight and the belt fits correctly
5 years old
60 pounds
10 years old
questions.explanation: A booster is required until the child is 4'9" tall or age eight and the adult belt fits properly.

questions.prompt: Oregon's seat belt law requires safety belts to be worn:
choices: In every available seating position
Only by the driver
Only in the front seat
Only on highways
questions.explanation: Oregon requires every driver and passenger to be properly restrained in all seating positions.

questions.prompt: When you park on any hill, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and turn your wheels appropriately
Leave the car in neutral with no brake
Leave the wheels straight
Leave the engine running
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and turning the wheels keeps the vehicle from rolling away on a slope.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for people with disabilities only if:
choices: You have a valid disabled parking permit or plate
You will be quick
No other spaces are open
You turn on your hazard lights
questions.explanation: Accessible spaces are reserved for vehicles displaying a valid disabled permit or plate.

questions.prompt: When parking parallel on a level street with no curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the edge of the road
Toward the center of the road
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning the wheels toward the road's edge keeps the car from rolling into traffic.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an Oregon instruction permit?
choices: 15 years old
14 years old
16 years old
18 years old
questions.explanation: You must be at least 15 to apply for an Oregon instruction permit.

questions.prompt: The Oregon knowledge test has how many questions?
questions.explanation: The Oregon Class C knowledge test consists of 35 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: To pass the Oregon knowledge test, you must answer at least how many questions correctly?
questions.explanation: You need at least 28 of 35 correct, which is about 80 percent, to pass.

questions.prompt: While driving on an Oregon instruction permit, you must be accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver 21 or older seated next to you
Any passenger over 18
A licensed driver in the back seat
No one, if you have passed the test
questions.explanation: Permit holders must always have a licensed driver age 21 or older in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: Under 18 applicants in Oregon must complete supervised driving of:
choices: 50 hours with an approved driver education course, or 100 hours without
10 hours
25 hours regardless of course
200 hours
questions.explanation: Teens need 50 supervised hours plus an approved course, or 100 supervised hours without the course.

questions.prompt: Before applying for a provisional license, an under-18 driver must hold the permit for at least:
choices: 6 months
3 months
1 month
1 year
questions.explanation: Oregon requires holding the instruction permit for at least six months before getting a provisional license.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with an Oregon provisional license, you may not carry:
choices: Passengers under 20 who are not immediate family
Any passengers at all
Passengers over 20
More than five passengers
questions.explanation: For the first six months, you may not carry passengers under age 20 unless they are immediate family.

questions.prompt: Oregon provisional drivers generally may not drive between:
choices: Midnight and 5 a.m.
10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
9 p.m. and 4 a.m.
Midnight and 7 a.m.
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers are restricted from driving between midnight and 5 a.m., with limited exceptions.

questions.prompt: Provisional license passenger and night restrictions last for:
choices: One year or until age 18, whichever comes first
Six months
Two years
Until age 21
questions.explanation: The restrictions end after one year or when the driver turns 18, whichever comes first.

questions.prompt: For supervised practice hours to count, the supervising driver must have been licensed for at least:
choices: 3 years
1 year
5 years
6 months
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must have held a license for at least three years for the practice hours to count.

questions.prompt: If you are stopped by police, you should:
choices: Pull over safely to the right, stay in the vehicle, and keep your hands visible
Get out and approach the officer
Continue to a well-lit area miles away
Reach into the glovebox immediately
questions.explanation: Pulling over promptly and keeping your hands visible keeps the stop safe for you and the officer.

questions.prompt: Oregon law requires every driver to carry:
choices: Liability insurance
Only collision insurance
No insurance if the car is paid off
Insurance only for new cars
questions.explanation: All Oregon drivers must carry liability insurance, and driving without it can bring fines and suspension.

questions.prompt: If you are in a crash that causes injury or significant property damage, you must:
choices: Stop, exchange information, render aid, and report as required
Leave if no police are present
Drive away if the other driver agrees
Only stop if your car is damaged
questions.explanation: You must stop, share information, help the injured, and report the crash; leaving the scene is a serious crime.

questions.prompt: When an officer asks, you must be able to show your:
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
License only
Birth certificate
Vehicle title
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and present a license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance on request.

questions.prompt: Violating provisional license restrictions can result in:
choices: Extending the restriction period and delaying full licensing
No penalty
An immediate full license
A warning only, never a penalty
questions.explanation: Breaking provisional restrictions can lengthen the restriction period and delay getting a full license.

questions.prompt: A DUII conviction in Oregon can result in:
choices: Fines, license suspension, and possible jail
Only a small fine
No license impact
A warning for first offenders
questions.explanation: DUII penalties include fines, license suspension, and potential jail, and they are harsher for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Refusing a breath test after a lawful DUII arrest leads to:
choices: An automatic license suspension under the Implied Consent law
No consequence
A reduced fine only
A short driving ban of one day
questions.explanation: Implied Consent means refusal results in an automatic suspension, separate from any criminal penalty.

questions.prompt: Serious or repeated traffic violations in Oregon can lead to:
choices: Suspension of your driving privileges
Automatic dismissal
A free defensive driving course only
No record being kept
questions.explanation: Accumulating serious or repeated violations can result in suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: Driving while your license is suspended in Oregon is:
choices: A serious offense with additional penalties
Allowed for short trips
Permitted for work only
A minor parking violation
questions.explanation: Driving on a suspended license is a serious violation that adds further penalties.

questions.prompt: Leaving the scene of a crash that caused injury is:
choices: A serious crime
Allowed if you call later
A minor traffic ticket
Permitted if the other driver is at fault
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of an injury crash (hit and run) is a serious criminal offense in Oregon.
